entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

Here is the Keynote from SAP:

Comment below your thoughts on TechEd.

entry.title: SAP User Experience News for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Q4/2023
entry.content:
Joule is now generally available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023. Joule saves you a lot of time when you need to refer to documentation, by giving you a concise summary and pointing you to relevant sections. It also helps you find the right application for well over one hundred navigational scenarios, as well as providing some transactional interactions for selected business objects. If you move up to Feature Pack Stack 2 (FPS2), you can also benefit from built-in collaboration, with the SAP Collaboration Manager now generally available with enhanced functionality. Talking about collaboration, FPS2 also enables users to initiate a chat or call directly from a contact card via Microsoft Teams integration.

This post covers:

  • Joule
  • New collaboration capabilities via:
    -  SAP Collaboration Manager
    -  Contact card integration with Microsoft Teams
  • SAP Mobile Start update
  • News for UI developers, web and mobile

If you want to see how good SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition now looks, across all the main product areas, then I thoroughly recommend that you have a look at the new online Product Tour.

In case you haven’t already seen these, do have a look at the user experience innovations which came with FPS00 and FPS01:

  • SAP User Experience Update: What’s New for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)
  • User Experience Advances with SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)

Joule

Users can save around ten minutes every time they need to refer to the documentation, by using Joule to give them a concise summary, created using generative AI. The response also provides three links to the most relevant sections in the documentation. We call this the Informational Pattern. Figure 1 below shows an example for someone working in sales, and Figure 2 shows an example for someone working in procurement.

Figure 1: Using Joule in sales to get help from the documentation, in this case asking “How can I make mass changes to sales orders?”. Alt Text: An image showing Joule on the left, with the query text and an eleven line response below it, followed by the option to expand to see the search results. On the right, an image of Joule is shown with the search results expanded to show three results, the first one called “Mass Changes of Sales Documents”, referring to the documentation of the app with that name. Each search result has a heading with three lines of explanatory text below it.

 

 

 

Figure 2: Using Joule in procurement to get help from the documentation, in this case asking “Can I configure a workflow for my purchase requisitions?”. Alt Text: An image showing Joule on the left, with the query text and a nine line response below it, followed by the option to expand to see the search results. On the right, an image of Joule is shown with the search results expanded to show three results, the first one called “How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Purc…”, referring to the documentation. Each search result has a heading with three lines of explanatory text below it.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023, users can also benefit from the navigational pattern for over one hundred use cases, in areas such as procurement, finance, sales, and service management. This pattern allows them to navigate directly to apps relevant to their query. You can find out more in this blog post:

  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

 Furthermore, we provide some selected transactional use cases in these areas too.

 To find out more about Joule, have a look at the documentation:

  • Joule in SAP Help Portal

Planned Innovations

As announced at SAP TechEd on October 8th, we have many plans for making Joule even more useful for users, as well as enabling you to build your own skills to extend what SAP delivers. As always, our plans are subject to change, but of course we hope to deliver as planned.

Some highlights are:

  • Joule for Mobile, built into SAP Mobile Start. As announced, this is planned to become available in November 2024.
  • Joule studio in SAP Build for building your own skills is planned to become available for beta testing later this year, and become generally available in Q1/2025. Get a preview by watching the recording of this TechEd talk:
    -  AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Beyond that, we are working on having many Joule agents interact with each other, across our products – making Joule agentic. Also, to support hands-free interaction, we are working on integration with Siri, so that users can use voice to interact.

 If you haven’t watched it live, then have a look at the replay of the TechEd talk by our AI leaders:

  • AI100 | Artificial intelligence that creates real-world results

New Collaboration Capabilities

With Feature Pack Stack 2 we introduce the following new collaboration capabilities.

SAP Collaboration Manager

SAP Collaboration Manager allows users to:

  • Take notes when using an application; these notes are pinned to the application, and are listed in the Relevant Chats tab when a user has that application open in the same window as the SAP Collaboration Manager.
  • Ad-hoc chat with colleagues, directly in the SAP system.
  • Share and annotate screenshots; a link is provided to the recipient with which they can navigate directly to the UI where the screenshot was taken.
  • Share the business context.
  • Upload and share files.

Figure 3 shows an example where a user Jones Davidson has created a chat with a colleague Alyssa Arnold and shared a screenshot along with a request to her to take a look at the two open sales orders in the screenshot.

For a closer look at some of the above features, watch it in action here in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editions 2308 system (what is shown is now available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02):

  • Video: SAP Collaboration Manager (2:10 minutes)

Figure 3: Using SAP Collaboration Manager to chat with a colleague and share a screenshot Alt Text: An image showing a list of three sales orders selected in the Manage Sales Orders app, and SAP Collaboration Manager as an overlay on the right showing the chat text and the screenshot image.

Beyond the above features, users can now also:

  • Mark their favorites, and filter by them (using the Favorites tab).
  • Set chats to unread.
  • Add and share insight cards from My Home.

These features are shown in Figure 4.

Figure 4: On the left: SAP Collaboration Manager offers tabs for “Recent chats”, “All Chats” and “Favorites”. You see the star indicating a favorite; the blue bar on the left of the second chat indicates that it has been set to unread. On the right: an insight card added to a chat. Alt Text: An image showing two screenshots of SAP Collaboration Manager. On the left, showing “All Chats” tab with a list of three chats. The second entry in the list is marked as unread, and has a star indicating it is a favorite. On the right, showing an insight card with a vertical bar chart embedded in the chat.

Contact card integration with Microsoft Teams

Users can now initiate a chat or call with Microsoft Teams directly from a contact card in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02. This feature is also available for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (on-premise) with FPS02.

Figure 5 shows two examples for this: using it directly on the My Home page to contact the creator of a task, and using it to contact a customer directly out of a sales order.

Figure 5: Two examples for how you can initiate a chat or call from a contact card via Microsoft Teams – from a To-Do card in My Home (upper image) and from a sold-to party link (lower image). Alt Text: An image showing two screenshots of popovers next to links referring to contacts -  for the ToDo card the contact is the creator of the task, for the other one it is the sold-to-party of the sales order. Each popover contains a section for initiating a Microsoft Teams collaboration with icons for chat, audio call and video call.

SAP Mobile Start

Users like to feel in charge, and a good way to support that is to let them define their favorites. The My Home page provides this feature and, as we saw above, the SAP Collaboration Manager has introduced this capability too.

Now also SAP Mobile Start lets users very easily define up to six favorites, two of which appear on the start screen next to a “See all” link to show all six. Figure 6 shows you what this looks like.

Figure 6: SAP Mobile Start lets users define their favorite apps / tiles. Alt Text: Three screenshots of SAP Mobile Start are shown. The one on the left shows the start page, which now has a “Favorites” section at the top, above the “Latest To-Dos” and “Insights” sections. The Favorites section shows two apps, each with a KPI shown as a number; on the right it has a “See all” link. The image in the middle shows all the favorites, in this case three are listed. The image on the right shows a list of all apps which can be selected as a favorite.

Find out more about this, as well as the new capability to upload and download attachments from tasks in this blog post:

  • SAP Mobile Start V1.12 - Release Update

October 16th update: SAP Mobile Start V2.0 is now available, supporting Spaces & Pages to group your applications; cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition; Situation Handling standard framework capabilities; and enhancements to task handling as well as optimized layouts for larger screens, as provided by foldables and tablets on Android. Check them out in this blog post:

  • SAP Mobile Start V2.0 - Release Update

News for UI Developers, Web and Mobile

The major news for UI developers for both web and mobile is that SAP Build now supports ABAP Cloud, and not just CAP (Cloud Application Programming model) for other programming languages such as Java and JavaScript. Get a comprehensive overview in this blog post from Michael Ameling:

  • Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!

Providing Joule skills is also a powerful way to enhance the user experience – I covered our main planned innovations there in already in the “Planned Innovations” part of the above Joule section.

News for Web Developers

Alt Text: Image of the white UI5 logo on a blue background with the text “UI5 linter 1.0 New Release” below it.

The main news is that UI5 linter 1.0 has now been released and is generally available. It is a static code analysis tool which helps ensure that you have good quality code, and which saves you a lot of time debugging later by catching issues up front. It can check for problems such as the use of deprecated UI5 libraries, framework APIs etc. as well as the usage of global variables and possible CSP violations. Find out more in this blot post:

  • Release of UI5 linter v1.0

I would also like to point you to the updated SAP BTP Developer’s Guide, where we have now brought in user experience design advice, as well as restructuring it to cover Explore, Discover, Design, Deliver and Scale & Run phases:

  • SAP BTP Developer’s Guide
  • If you read the guide, do spend a few minutes to give us feedback, so that we can improve it even further, by taking this survey to help us improve the guide!

The developer’s guide recommends using SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori elements, built on OData services, for developing UIs. Should you not be able to follow this recommendation because you have existing applications using different protocols or frameworks, we have options to help you nevertheless:

  • If you have existing back-end code which cannot provide OData services, we have now released the SAPUI5 meta-data controls library sap.ui.mdc, as outlined here:
    -  Smart choices for your UI: When to use sap.ui.mdc
  • If you already have applications written with a different UI technology, such as Angular or React, we have released Version 2.0 of our UI5 Web Components:
    -  Blast Off! UI5 Web Components 2.0 is Now Live!

For friends of ABAP, we recently introduced the ability to create Fiori elements apps directly from within the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Find out more here:

  • How to create a SAP Fiori Elements application from within ADT?
  • SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools

News for Mobile Developers

The main news for mobile developers is the latest releases of the mobile SDKs, as well as the integration of the Mobile Development Kit into SAP Build Code. Here are links to find out more:

  • How to Develop SAP MDK Applications in SAP Build Code
  • Streamline SAP MDK Development with the Power of Generative AI and Joule
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS 24.8.0 is now available
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android 24.8.0 is now available

For a deeper dive, watch the recordings of the Devtoberfest sessions on developing mobile apps:

  • Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK (50:40 minutes)
  • Craft Enterprise Apps for iOS, iPadOS, and visionOS with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (47:16 minutes)
  • Build Elegant Enterprise Apps for Android with SAP Fiori and SAP Build Code (45:24 minutes)

Final Words

I hope you are as excited about these new user experience innovations as I am! FPS02 brings quite a few additional new features beyond user experience, discover all of them here:

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS02

If you like what you see here, we encourage you to upgrade to FPS02, so that you can run your business even better with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023.

entry.title: Where can I start learning to configure and integrate Joule, SAP’s AI copilot?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Provisioning and Implementing Joule learning journey

Overview
This learning journey offers a comprehensive approach to understanding, configuring, and integrating Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, across various SAP platforms, including SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. It will guide you through both the functional aspects of leveraging Joule to boost productivity for your customers and the technical configurations needed to set it up effectively.

Learning objectives

After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Master fundamental functionalities of Joule in the SAP landscape
  • Set up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Understand how to commercialize Joule for customers
  • Troubleshoot common setup issues
  • Customize Joule to meet specific business needs
  • Stay updated with the developments in Joule through ongoing learning and community engagement

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

 Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule
entry.content:
SAP Build Code, which focuses on pro-code development, offers a variety of GenAI functionalties to support the development process using CAP, HANA development, as well as UI5.

In this blog post, I'll focus on the freestyle aspect of UI5 development, demonstrating how to get started with a freestyle UI5 application. My aim is to showcase how Joule can be used to both accelerate the development and provide support for those who may not be UI5 experts to the very fingertips.

The following simple scenario will give you a glimpse into developing applications with SAP Build Code. Using the BTP Trial version you can currently setup a SAP Build Code instance to try Joule’s GenAI capabilities.

 

Getting Started

  1. Open the SAP Build Lobby from the BTP Subaccount Cockpit under Services > Instances and Subscriptions > SAP Build Code.
  2. Create a new SAP Build Code Fiori, Full-Stack, or Mobile application. For this scenario, a SAP Fiori Application is suitable.
  3. Enter a project name. If no dev space exists, it will be prefilled to create a new Fiori dev space:

4. Once the project is created and the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) dev space starts, click on the project name to open BAS.

 

Creating a UI5 Application

Create a UI5 application using the template wizard in BAS:

  1. Go to Get Started > New Project from Template > SAP Fiori Generator
  2. Use the Basic template to start from scratch
  3. For the OData service, create a destination to the OData V2 as an example OData connection
  4.  Add a view name – In my case Main
  5. Optionally, set the deployment configuration and add the app to the managed application router if you want to deploy and consume the app from within SAP Build Work Zone

 

Leveraging Joule for UI5 Development

Recently, major UX updates were introduced to the Joule capabilities, moving from mainly being able to access Joule from the Guide Center towards a single chat, where users can use different slash commands to invoke tailored Joule functionalities.

For UI5 questions and development the /ui5 slash command is there to achieve this:

 

Example: Creating a Product Table

In the yet empty project, in order to see the /ui5 slash command, we firstly need to open the controller or view that was created from the template. Afterwards, the /ui5 slash command is suggested and we can directly start with a sample prompt to show products of the OData service:

"/ui5 create a table in the main view that displays the products of the northwind service. Provide data bindings to the properties of this odata entity."

After invoking the prompt, Joule already looked up files considered important for executing the task:

In the answer, Joule provided the code in the Main.view.xml including the product table. Since the screenshot of the whole response is large and thus difficult to paste here, this is the code it provided :

 

<mvc:View controllerName="demoapp.controller.Main"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true"
    xmlns="sap.m">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <content>
            <Table id="productTable" items="{
                path: '/Products'
            }">
                <headerToolbar>
                    <Toolbar>
                        <Title text="Products" />
                    </Toolbar>
                </headerToolbar>
                <columns>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Product ID" />
                    </Column>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Product Name" />
                    </Column>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Quantity Per Unit" />
                    </Column>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Unit Price" />
                    </Column>
                </columns>
                <items>
                    <ColumnListItem>
                        <cells>
                            <Text text="{ProductID}" />
                            <Text text="{ProductName}" />
                            <Text text="{QuantityPerUnit}" />
                            <Text text="{UnitPrice}" />
                        </cells>
                    </ColumnListItem>
                </items>
            </Table>
        </content>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

As you can see, the suggestions looked good, also containing data binding to 4 properties of the Products entity. That already saves some manually coding time for developers and can be further adapted to include more columns and properties.

An explanation at the end is also provided:

Afterwards, the application can be previewed using the green play button in BAS for example. The result already looks promising:

 

Formatting and adding Functionality

Now, I wanted to format the Unit Price to make it look a little bit nicer and also display  the sign of the Euro currency after the number. Additionally, I wanted to create a custom column with some sort of action that can be invoked on each product. So, Joule was asked again:

"/ui5 format the unit price in the table to round to 2 decimals and include an euro sign after the number. Create a formatter file with a function for this. Additionally, include another column in the table named Order where each cell is composed of a button that indicated that the user wants to buy this product. Provide a basic press event handler one of this button is pressed."

Multiple files were suggested to be adapted and/or changed, such as creating a formatter.js file to format the UnitPrice property.

On top of that, it created a column Order, a function onPressOrder in the Main.controller.js file, and a press event in the Main.view.xml in order to show an UI5 MessageToast of the order placed for a certain product:

 

To further add some application logic, Joule assisted to create a condition when clicking the button:

“/ui5 please change the onOrderPress function that checks if the unit price of the product is above 50 €. If this is the case, show a message dialog indicating that the products above 50 € can currently not be ordered.“

Result, only when clicking a product over 50 (€):

 

Using AI-Powered Code Completion

Besides Joule, since very recently SAP Build Code introduced code completion and suggestions directly in the editor. Therefore, we can also make use of this AI feature.In order to show this, I added a comment in the onOrderPress code and described that I want to have a confirmation dialog instead of a message toast. Shortly after, a code suggestion was directly available in the function:

I had to remove the MessageToast line of code, but besides that, it immediately worked:

 

Understanding your Code with Joule

Coming to the end, one more thing to highlight: Using Joule to help explaining code. As such, the following prompt was sent: 

“/ui5 what does the main view do?”

Here is an extract of Joule’s answer, which helps to understand the code even further:

 

Conclusion

As you can see, SAP Build Code with Joule supports a variety of use cases for UI5 development, in addition to its other GenAI focus on CAP and Full-Stack development. Please keep in mind that prompt responses can vary, thus potentially leading to slighty different code proposals. Nonetheless, it's a powerful feature that can enhance your development process, whether you're an experienced UI5 developer or just getting started. Let’s see what the future brings with more and more features getting introduced!

entry.title: 5 Major Generative AI Innovations Unveiled at SAP TechEd 2024
entry.content:
At SAP TechEd 2024, SAP revealed several key innovations centered around Generative AI, further enhancing its ecosystem and offering businesses powerful tools to drive digital transformation. These new capabilities focus on simplifying complex processes, boosting customization, and integrating AI into the core of enterprise applications. Here are the five major Generative AI announcements that will shape the future of SAP’s intelligent enterprise solutions.

1. Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud in SAP Build

One of the standout announcements is the introduction of the Generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud, embedded within SAP Build. This new toolkit allows users to execute data analysis and perform advanced machine learning tasks effortlessly.

By using the AI-powered capabilities available through Joule—SAP’s intelligent assistant—users can now complete complex tasks like generating time-series forecast models using natural language prompts. This eliminates the need for deep technical knowledge in machine learning, making AI accessible to business users and enabling faster, data-driven decision-making.

2. AI-Driven Optimization in SAP Integration Suite

SAP’s Integration Suite now comes equipped with Generative AI-driven optimization for custom scripts. This feature allows users to open the script editor and automatically receive AI-generated recommendations to optimize their code.

This enhancement empowers developers and business users alike by reducing manual effort and improving efficiency. The AI-driven optimizations make integration tasks smoother and more reliable, enhancing the overall performance of custom solutions.

3. Joule Studio: Design Custom AI Skills with Low-Code Tools

Joule Studio, a new feature within SAP Build, offers a low-code platform for creating custom AI skills that integrate seamlessly with your SAP landscape. With Joule Studio, developers can build intelligent applications without extensive coding knowledge, using a simplified interface.

For instance, if you need to bring ServiceNow tickets into Joule, you can easily create a custom skill using Joule Studio. The platform makes it simple to configure API parameters, deploy the skill, and instantly retrieve data from within Joule, making it an incredibly efficient solution for businesses looking to streamline their workflows and automate tasks.

Joule Studio will enter beta later this year, with general availability expected shortly after.

4. Enhanced Generative AI Hub: New Models and Capabilities

SAP also introduced a major upgrade to the Generative AI Hub, a platform that integrates state-of-the-art AI models with SAP applications. The hub now supports closed and open-source models from leading providers like Meta and Mistral, enabling businesses to harness the best of both foundational models and real-time data from SAP systems.

Key enhancements include:
- Orchestration services for managing AI workflows.
- Data masking and content filtering capabilities for improved privacy and security.
- New prompt templates and prompt management tools, making it easier to deploy AI models and create custom solutions.

Developers will also have access to new SDKs for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, ensuring a seamless integration of AI into a variety of SAP environments.

5. SAP Knowledge Graph: Unlocking AI-Powered Insights

SAP’s Knowledge Graph, combined with the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, is set to revolutionize how businesses interact with SAP data. The Knowledge Graph provides a vast repository of structured metadata, including over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views, and more than 7 million properties and fields.

By linking Large Language Models (LLMs) with the semantic model of SAP, the Knowledge Graph enables powerful use cases, such as:
- AI-driven predictions and insights.
- Advanced information retrieval capabilities.
- Enhanced analytics across the SAP landscape.

This comprehensive dataset serves as a bridge between natural language processing (NLP) and SAP’s structured data, opening up new opportunities for AI-driven innovation. The SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine will be available in Q1 2025, offering developers a robust foundation for building advanced business applications.

Why These Announcements Matter

SAP’s focus on Generative AI at TechEd 2024 highlights a clear shift toward making AI more accessible to all users—whether they’re business professionals, developers, or data scientists. These innovations are designed to reduce the complexity of working with AI while enhancing its ability to solve real-world business problems.

With the introduction of tools like the Generative AI Toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud and Joule Studio, users can now build intelligent, AI-driven applications with ease. The Generative AI Hub and SAP Knowledge Graph further empower developers to leverage cutting-edge models and tap into SAP’s vast structured data for advanced analytics and business insights.

As these tools become available, businesses can expect a significant boost in their ability to automate processes, gain actionable insights, and create smarter, more efficient solutions in the SAP ecosystem.

Stay tuned for the full release of these features in the coming months, as SAP continues to lead the charge in bringing AI to enterprise systems in powerful and accessible ways.

entry.title: Accelerate AI-Powered Insights with Joule
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced, data-driven world, staying ahead requires more than just access to data - it demands the ability to harness that data effectively. Enter Joule, a game-changing AI solution designed to deliver faster, smarter, and more impactful decision-making. With Joule, organizations can seamlessly integrate AI into their existing SAP systems, transforming raw data into actionable insights that drive innovation and efficiency.

Getting Started with SAP Joule
But how do you get started with SAP Joule? For those looking to elevate their SAP skills and implement AI-powered solutions, the journey begins with the right training. Before diving into a full Joule implementation, it's crucial to build a solid foundation.

Build Your Foundation: Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio
The Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio course is a free, self-paced learning journey that introduces core AI concepts within the SAP ecosystem. This course provides a comprehensive understanding of how SAP leverages AI, preparing you to maximize the value of Joule and other AI solutions.

Master Joule: Provisioning and Implementing Joule
Once you've laid the groundwork, the next step is the Provisioning and Implementing Joule learning journey. Designed for IT professionals and SAP users alike, this course equips you with the knowledge and skills to effectively provision, deploy, and implement Joule. By completing this journey, you'll not only learn how to unlock the full potential of Joule in driving analytics and operational efficiency but also earn a Record of Achievement, showcasing your understanding of Joule’s capabilities.

 Incorporating Joule into your organization is a powerful way to enhance decision-making, streamline processes, and stay competitive in an AI-powered future. Ready to take the next step? Start your learning journey today and transform how your organization approaches insights and efficiency with SAP Joule.                          

Benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on the SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Enabling More Secure and Responsible Generative AI: Key Insights from SAP Teched
entry.content:
The recent SAP TechEd session, presented by @BettinaLaugwitz and Franziska Dobrigkeit, shed light on how SAP is leading the way in ensuring the security and responsibility of generative AI. In an era where AI technologies are becoming omnipresent, SAP is embedding robust ethical guidelines and governance frameworks into its AI processes. 

The Pillars of Responsible AI

Their approach to responsible AI is anchored in three key pillars: AI Safety and Ethics, AI Security, and AI Compliance.

1. AI Safety and Ethics

At the heart of responsible AI lies a commitment to ethical standards and fair outcomes. This involves ensuring that AI systems are designed to promote inclusivity and mitigate biases that can lead to discrimination. As generative AI often learns from uncurated datasets, there’s a pressing need to actively counteract the encoding of harmful patterns within AI models.

SAP’s approach to AI ethics revolves around four key requirements: human agency and oversight, fairness and nondiscrimination, transparency and explainability, and privacy and data governance. The AI Safety and Ethics requirements mandate safeguarding human autonomy through a human-in/on-the-loop approach, ensuring AI systems do not perpetuate marginalization or discrimination, prioritizing transparency and explainability in AI decisions, and protecting privacy while maintaining data integrity. These principles help create AI systems that are trustworthy and equitable.

2. AI Security

As businesses increasingly rely on cloud-based solutions, SAP emphasizes the importance of robust AI security measures. This includes implementing security controls specifically tailored for SAP’s cloud solutions, ensuring that sensitive data remains protected from threats.

Security is paramount in SAP’s AI framework, particularly for cloud solutions. SAP ensures the security of their cloud solutions from SAP and SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) by implementing rigorous AI security controls. These controls protect the data and processes pivotal to AI model training, ensuring that AI solutions are not only effective but also secure from external threats and vulnerabilities.

3. AI Compliance

The evolving landscape of AI regulations, such as the EU AI Act and the US Executive Order 14110, requires organizations to stay ahead of compliance requirements. SAP’s commitment to regulatory adherence ensures that its AI initiatives not only meet legal standards but also align with ethical norms. Compliance with legal and regulatory standards is the third pillar of SAP’s responsible AI strategy. SAP applies stringent checks through audits and regulatory compliance processes to ensure their AI models and data usage meet international standards. They manage data for model training with utmost scrutiny, ensuring compliance with regional and industry-specific regulations.

AI Safety and Ethics

The foundation of SAP’s responsible AI strategy lies in their comprehensive safety and ethical guidelines. SAP’s commitment to AI safety and ethics is divided into internal governance and operationalization on one hand, and adherence to external influences and standards on the other. Internally, the SAP Executive Board, supported by the SAP AI Global Ethics Steering Committee and the AI Ethics Office, drives ethical governance. They rely on guiding principles such as the UNESCO recommendation on the ethics of AI, ensuring these are reflected in product standards and a trustworthy AI workstream.

Externally, SAP aligns with crucial ethical standards and recommendations from organizations like UNESCO, the OECD, IEEE, and more. They adhere to responsible AI regulations like the EU AI Act and the US Executive Order 14110 and take guidance from the SAP AI Global Ethics Advisory Panel. This dual approach ensures that SAP’s AI innovations are ethically sound and globally compliant.

The AI Ethics Assessment Process

SAP’s AI ethics assessment process delineates how AI use cases are evaluated for potential risks and compliance. This process, as depicted in the flowchart image above, involves impact assessments, categorization of cases as redline, high-risk, or standard, and subsequent steps to ensure adherence to SAP's AI ethics policies.

Implementing Responsible AI

SAP has established a comprehensive assessment process to evaluate AI initiatives against key ethical criteria:

  • Human Oversight: Ensuring human agency in AI decision-making processes to maintain autonomy.
  • Fairness and Non-Discrimination: Actively working to prevent biases from being perpetuated in AI outputs.
  • Transparency and Explainability: Striving for clarity in how AI systems are developed and how they operate, even when dealing with complex models.
  • Privacy and Data Governance: Maintaining strict protocols to protect individual privacy and the integrity of data.

Innovative Solutions for Ethical AI

To operationalize these ethical principles, SAP has introduced several innovative solutions:

Orchestration: By designing streamlined AI workflows that connect various components, SAP enables organizations to focus on innovation without getting bogged down in integration challenges. This ensures that AI workflows are powerful yet manageable, connecting diverse components like data pipelines and AI models, as illustrated in the orchestration pipeline flowchart. Orchestration minimizes maintenance while maximizing the efficiency of AI deployment.

  • Grounding: Enhancing the contextual relevance of AI outputs by enriching requests with pertinent business data, reducing the likelihood of inaccurate or misleading responses. This ensures that AI workflows are powerful yet manageable, connecting diverse components like data pipelines and AI models. Orchestration minimizes maintenance while maximizing the efficiency of AI deployment.

  • Data Masking: Protecting sensitive information while retaining the necessary context for AI applications, thereby mitigating biases and safeguarding user privacy. A crucial measure helping to avoid bias and privacy breaches.

  • Content Filtering: Implementing filters to screen out harmful or biased outputs, ensuring that generative AI remains a safe and trustworthy tool for users. This module allows filtering undesired prompts and responses, thereby avoiding biased, harmful, or unethical AI outputs.

As the landscape of AI continues to evolve, fostering responsible innovation will be essential to build trust and ensure a positive impact on society.

SAP’s approach to responsible and secure generative AI, guided by key principles and stringent frameworks, demonstrates a robust model for ethical AI deployment. By combining internal governance with adherence to external standards, SAP not only pioneers in AI innovation but also ensures these advancements are ethical, secure, and compliant. The insights from the SAP TechEd session offer a comprehensive understanding of how SAP addresses the critical challenges of AI ethics and governance, providing a blueprint for the future of responsible AI.

entry.title: Harness retrieval-augmented generation in Joule and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Imagine if you could converse with your own business data, leveraging the human-like text responses of a Large Language Model (LLM), and have access to context-aware information to make well-informed decisions. We're excited to introduce a central capability at SAP that grounds LLM responses in business data and provides users with relevant, reliable, and responsible output tailored to their specific needs. By feeding relevant and up-to-date information to an LLM, we reduce model hallucinations - all without the need to finetune the model. 

SAP leverages retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to deliver a central and domain-agnostic service that is easy to use and scales across all business functions. This service also fact-checks LLM-generated statements by tracing information back to its sources, enabling users to act with confidence based on verified data. 

In this blog, let’s dive into two capabilities:  

  • Document grounding, a RAG feature for Joule, SAP’s AI copilot 
  • The Grounding capability in the orchestration module of Generative AI hub to build custom AI applications 

Document grounding: Enhancing the Q&A feature of Joule  

The Document grounding capability allows customers to use their own unstructured or semi-structured documents to leverage the Q&A feature of Joule. By connecting their own repositories, such as SharePoint, customers can gain fast and reliable insights from their business documents, saving time spent on finding, opening, and reading documents. 

Real-world use-case: SAP SuccessFactors is a perfect example of how Document grounding can revolutionize employee self-service capabilities via Joule. Let's say an employee needs to find information on their company's parental leave policy. Instead of sifting through lengthy documents, the employee simply asks Joule: "What is the maximum entitlement for parental leave?" Joule, powered by the Document grounding service, retrieves the relevant information from their company's HR policy documents (previously indexed) and provides them with a concise, accurate answer.

View of the Q&A feature in Joule, enhanced with Document Grounding 

 

Business Impact: This capability offers an intuitive user experience, improves self-service efficiency, and reduces the total cost of ownership (TCO) with 24x7 HR support. It's not just an AI feature; it's a whole new way of empowering teams with knowledge at their fingertips. It can lead to potential efficiency improvements of 70% reduction in employee time needed to investigate HR policies & 81% reduction in time spent by HR on ticket handling, showcasing the tangible impact of this capability. 

But the benefits extend beyond just HR. Imagine a sales executive quickly accessing the latest product specifications, or a finance leader getting real-time insights into company financial policies. By making information more accessible and actionable, Document grounding has the potential to transform decision-making processes across the entire organization.  

What’s under the hood? 

SAP’s Document grounding service leverages RAG to add vital context to LLM responses through a multi-step indexing & retrieval process: 

  1. Indexing Pipeline: This component ingests data from diverse sources, processes it, and splits it into manageable chunks. It then generates embeddings of this data and stores them in the SAP HANA Vector Database. 
  2. Vector Database: The SAP HANA Vector Engine queries these vector embeddings. 
  3. Retrieval: For every user query, vector embeddings are generated using the LLM model and a search in the HANA Vector Database finds relevant text chunks. 

Find more technical and commercial information about Document Grounding in our dedicated Help Portal page. 

A Grounding capability via generative AI hub for your customized AI applications 

While Document grounding is meant to cover Joule scenarios, SAP is also addressing the needs of developer persona who would want to benefit from the RAG capability within their customized AI applications. 

By the end of 2024, the document grounding capability will be fully integrated in the orchestration module of the generative AI hub, alongside capabilities like prompt templating, data masking, and content filter. The mechanism exposed sooner in this article will be applicable via the consumption of the Orchestration service or direct API consumption (indexing and retrieval APIs).  

 Architecture of the Grounding capability inside Generative AI hub

What’s coming next? 

Among many other features, our roadmap through 2025 includesmulti-language support, contextualization, extended list of supported document types and formats as well as documents repositories.  

Also, to enhance the quality of indexing and retrieval, SAP is working on backstage technical layers like Automatic RAG (choosing the best chunking strategy automatically), advanced retrieval techniques, Knowledge Graph integration which comes preloaded with a comprehensive representation of SAP’s business entities (including ABAP tables, CDS views, APIs, and key data models), and will play a critical role in grounding AI models and enabling them to deliver more reliable, context-aware insights.  

Any questions? Feel free to contact Grounding product management.  

Email: sebastien.beghelli@sap.com 

Interested in learning more? Refer to the links below 

  • TechEd 2024 session about Document Grounding and the Grounding capability inside GenAI Hub
  • [SAP BTP Onboarding Series]Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - set up guide
  • Document grounding Discovery center page

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 Announcements - Consolidated and Simplified
entry.content:
In this blog, I have consolidated everything related to SAP TechEd 2024 in one place! Hope this will help the community.

Note: Don't miss the list of all the links of news, recordings, source-code, demo etc. towards the end of this blog.

 

First let's have a quick summary of all the key announcements.

 

 

Let's go in detail.

SAP Build + ABAP Cloud

  • SAP Build unifies the entire development and extension capabilities and will now also include ABAP Cloud.
  • Technically, it means that SAP Build will integrate ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP BTP.
  • Developers will also be able to create and monitor their ABAP Cloud projects directly from SAP Build.
  • It will be available by Q4 2024.
  • SAP Build Lobby will be the central starting point for all extension projects.

Demo of SAP Build Lobby

 

SAP Build for S/4HANA Extension Development

  • A new extensibility wizard is available now which will let developers access SAP Build directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • With this, developers can extend custom fields, business logic and processes either on-stack (deployed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP environment) or side-by-side (deployed in SAP BTP).
  • In Q4 2024, the extensibility wizard will let developers create custom SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build.

 

New Generative AI Capabilities of SAP Build with Joule

  • New capabilities in SAP Build Code such as code explanation and documentation search will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers (Q4 2024).
  • Developers will also be able to automate workflows in SAP Build Process Automation with assistance from generative AI (Q4 2024)
  • ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with SAP’s ABAP Cloud Development model, and also generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy codebases and migrate to a clean core (Q1 2025)
  • Developers will also be able to enhance Joule with custom skills using Joule studio in SAP Build, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications (Q1 2025)

For more information on SAP Build, refer to SAP Build.

 

Joule with SAP Build Work Zone

  • Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone (Q4 2024)
  • It will be available in SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, the SAP Start site and the SAP Mobile Start app.

 

Joule with Collaborative AI Agents

  • SAP will infuse Joule with multiple autonomous AI agents that will combine their unique expertise to collaboratively accomplish complex workflows (Q4 2024)
  • With an in-built agentic capability, Joule can now solve complex business problems for you through planning, self-reflection, logical reasoning and collaboration.
  • Developers will be able to extend Joule by building their own autonomous AI agent use cases with SAP Build (Q1 2025)

Joule Agent Demo

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

  • Joule will be available for all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers (Q4 2024)
  • Joule capabilities will expand to support key functions including sales professionals, warehouse clerks and project managers (Q1 of 2025)

 

Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  • Following the introduction of Joule into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in July, SAP launched a feature pack with new use cases that let business users instruct Joule with simple commands.
  • The feature pack includes a new implementation wizard that installs a model outlining criteria to evaluate extensions built with low-code, no-code and pro-code tools on SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP.
  • Joule will be incorporated into the top 300 most-frequently-used SAP Fiori apps (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will also be able to take master data change requests within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, so users don’t have to navigate across multiple menus.

 

Joule in SAP BTP

  • Joule will be available to use with SAP HANA Cloud in SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be incorporated into SAP BTP cockpit (Q4 2024)
  • Joule can be directed to perform transactional scenarios like creating a subaccount, onboarding users, and answering questions about SAP BTP administrations in general. 

 

Joule in Other SAP Solutions

  • Joule will be available in the SAP Service Cloud solution and SAP Concur solutions (Q1 2025)
  • SAP now offers Joule across the SAP Integrated Business Planning solution, the SAP Digital Manufacturing solution, and the SAP Asset Performance Management application.
  • SAP will continue to roll out Joule across the entire SAP Supply Chain portfolio.
  • Joule will be available in the SAP Sales Cloud solution with navigational and task-management assistance (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will help SAP LeanIX solutions users manage and optimize their IT landscape (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available with SAP Signavio (Q4 2024)
  • Joule will be available in SAP Mobile Start (Q4 2024)      

 

Joule studio in SAP Build

  • SAP will release Joule studio in SAP Build (Q4 2024).
  • Joule studio will provide a dedicated environment for businesses to create, deploy, monitor and manage custom skills for Joule developed with low-code, no-code capabilities.
  • Custom skills, which complement Joule’s out-of-the-box capabilities from SAP, extend conversational AI to organization-specific workflows.
  • Additionally, customers can now integrate any third-party system into Joule for an entirely integrated conversational user experience.

 

SAP Knowledge Graph

  • SAP announces the addition of a knowledge graph engine to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • The knowledge graph engine will let users identify and understand complex relationships in their data not otherwise discoverable using traditional data modeling tools.
  • SAP announces SAP Knowledge Graph, a new solution that will unlock the full value of SAP data by connecting it with the rich business context captured in SAP applications.
  • Accessible through SAP Datasphere and Joule in Q1 2025, it will help root AI in SAP-specific business semantics, reducing the risk of inaccurate or irrelevant results.
  • In its initial release, this solution will focus on SAP S/4HANA, providing a semantic layer that connects over 450,000 ABAP tables, 80,000 CDS views and thousands of SAP Fiori applications, along with OData APIs and entity sets.

 

SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine

  • Knowledge graph engine will be added to SAP HANA Cloud (Q1 2025)
  • By combining the vector engine and the knowledge graph engine, you can harness the power of structured and unstructured data together.

 

SAP Expands Strategic AI Partnerships

  • Anthropic: SAP introduces Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet in Amazon Bedrock to generative AI hub.
  • HCLTech: HCLTech announces a newly built AI Center of Excellence in Munich focused on developing AI use cases for the AI Foundation on SAP BTP.
  • IBM: SAP announces the addition of IBM Granite, IBM’s flagship series of transparent and efficient LLMs, to the growing repository of AI models available in generative AI hub.
  • Meta: SAP continues to execute its multi-vendor strategy for LLMs with the addition of Meta Llama 3.1 70B, which is built to deliver performance and cost efficiency.
  • Mistral AI: SAP continues to expand its partnership with Mistral AI by adding Mistral Large 2 to generative AI hub.
  • PwC: PwC announces it is using generative AI capabilities based on SAP’s generative AI hub.

 

Generative AI Hub + ABAP

  • Developers will be able to access generative AI hub capabilities from custom ABAP applications using the ABAP AI SDK powered by the intelligent scenario lifecycle management for SAP S/4HANA Cloud functionality. 
  • Using the ABAP AI SDK, ABAP developers can easily infuse AI capabilities into their custom applications and extensions.

 

Generative AI Hub – New LLMs Support

New LLMs will be available for generative AI hub by the end of 2024, including:

  • Aleph Alpha Pharia-1
  • Amazon Titan Image Generator
  • IBM Granite
  • Mistral Large 2
  • OpenAI Dall-E 3

 

Generative AI Hub – New Data Centers

New data centers for generative AI hub by the end of Q4 2024:

  • AWS: Singapore
  • Google Cloud Platform: Australia (Sydney); Japan (Osaka)
  • Microsoft Azure: Australia (Sydney); Switzerland (Zurich)

 

Generative AI Hub – Other Updates

  • We will be able to integrate advanced AI capabilities into web apps with new SDK support for ABAP, Java, and JavaScript in generative AI hub (Q4 2024)
  • Orchestration improvements to the generative AI hub capability in SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad that will enable document grounding and indexing pipelines (Q4 2024)

 

New generative AI functionality for customers move to the cloud faster

  • SAP announced a new generative AI functionality that helps customers move to the cloud faster (Q1 2025).
  • Accessed through the SAP Cloud ALM solution, customers and consultants will be able to get expert answers, proactive guidance, and task automation aligned with the RISE with SAP Methodology.

 

SAP Expands its Portfolio of Learning and Certification Offerings

  • SAP launches new role-based certifications, free training materials and hands-on opportunities for developers and tech professionals.
  • These includes 60 new self-paced learning journeys, trial experiences and solution demonstrations that help tech professionals benefit from the latest fully integrated and practical training.

 

SAP Datasphere

  • SAP announced new embedded data lake capabilities for SAP Datasphere (Q4 2024)
  • Built on SAP HANA Cloud SQL on files and data lake file service capabilities, it will expand the business data fabric architecture with a data lake option, complementing existing storage solutions.
  • This helps businesses access, manage, and analyze data across cloud and hybrid environments while preserving its context and logic, improving decision-making, operational efficiency, and AI-driven innovation.

 

New embedded data lake capabilities

  • An integrated object store, which provides a simpler way to store large amounts of data, making it easier for businesses to expand their storage as needed.
  • Spark compute, which facilitates efficient data transformation and processing based on existing SAP Datasphere data integration capabilities.
  • SQL on files functionality, which lets developers access data in the integrated object store without needing to physically copy the data.

These capabilities let users import and integrate data at scale while maintaining a consistent user experience that ensures seamless data integration from both SAP sources (including SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Warehouse) and non-SAP sources while preserving the original structure and context of the data.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

  • SAP announced general availability of the new SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature (Q1 2025)
  • SAP Analytics Cloud compass feature can be used to model complex risk scenarios and simulate a broad range of probable outcomes.

 

SAP Integration Suite

  • SAP Integration Suite, starter edition will offer an unlimited number of prepackaged SAP integrations and a fixed number of customizable integrations for GROW with SAP and SAP SuccessFactors customers (Q4 2024)
  • New integration adapters will allow users manage data from various third-party technology platforms, including Anaplan, Coupa, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more.
  • AI supported custom script optimization (2025)
  • AI support iFlow creation is now Generally Available
